title: Redirect / Hosted Checkout Handler — Schema Bundle

PaymentMethodRedirect.description: Configuration for the Redirect / Hosted Checkout handler. The hosted page is entirely managed by the payment provider; ACP only needs enough configuration to identify the merchant and declare available methods as a hint.

merchant_id.description: Merchant identifier assigned by the payment provider

merchant_name.description: Display name shown in checkout UI

supported_methods.description: Hint to the platform about payment methods available on the hosted page. Values are provider-defined strings. The hosted page ultimately controls method selection and availability.

Credential.description: Credential for a hosted checkout session. Encodes the minimal information ACP needs to send the buyer to the hosted page and receive an outcome: a URL to open, a session identifier, and return endpoints. Provider-specific fields (prefill data, retry configuration, analytics) are provider implementation details and do not belong in this schema.

checkout_url.description: HTTPS URL of the provider-hosted checkout page. The ACP runtime opens this URL via openExternal(). The merchant backend is responsible for constructing this URL, including any provider-specific query parameters.

session_id.description: Opaque session identifier assigned by the provider. Used to correlate this checkout session when complete_checkout is called.

return_url.description: HTTPS URL the provider redirects the buyer to after a successful payment

cancel_url.description: HTTPS URL the provider redirects the buyer to on abandonment or cancellation

Instrument.description: Payment instrument for the Redirect / Hosted Checkout handler

handler_id.description: Reference to the handler configuration ID

Error.description: Error response for redirect checkout requests

code.description: Provider-defined error code for programmatic handling

param.description: JSONPath of the offending field, if applicable

supported_versions.description: Supported API versions, newest first. Only included in version-related errors.

example.message: Merchant ID not found or inactive.
